title: Fermented Lovely Greek Tomato Stuffed Grape Leaves
description: A fermented, springy, and festive Greek dish featuring San-Marzano tomatoes, slow-cooked to perfection. This dish is a satvik-friendly twist on traditional dolmades.
instructions: Combine grape leaves, rice, chopped onion, garlic, lemon juice, olive oil, smoked paprika, and salt in a large bowl. Mix well to combine.
Add coarsely chopped San Marzano tomatoes to the bowl and mix until just combined.
Stuff each grape leaf with about 1 tablespoon of the mixture, folding the stem end over the filling and rolling the leaf into a tight cylinder.
Place the stuffed grape leaves seam-side down in a large saucepan or Dutch oven, packing them in tightly.
Combine yeast, water, and 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a small bowl, mixing to dissolve the yeast. Pour the yeast mixture over the grape leaves in the saucepan.
Cover the saucepan with a lid and let the mixture ferment at room temperature (about 70-75°F) for 2-3 days, or until the grape leaves are slightly softened and the filling is fragrant.
Once the fermentation is complete, carefully remove the stuffed grape leaves from the saucepan and place them on a wire rack set over a rimmed baking sheet.
Serve the dolmades chilled or at room temperature, garnished with additional lemon wedges and olive oil if desired.
To store, place the dolmades in an airtight container and refrigerate for up to 5 days or freeze for up to 3 months.

Note: If you prefer a more intense flavor, you can let the dolmades ferment for an additional day or two, but be careful not to over-ferment, as this can cause the grape leaves to become too soft and mushy.
